Large-Scale Analysis: GDS and PLINK BED Backends

Source: vignettes/LDxBlocks-large-scale.Rmd
LDxBlocks-large-scale.Rmd

All code chunks in this vignette have eval = FALSE because they require either optional packages (SeqArray, BEDMatrix) or large data files that are not shipped with the package. Copy and adapt them to your own data.

The scale problem

A 10 M-marker, 5,000-individual genotype matrix occupies roughly 200 GB as a dense R double matrix. Loading it into RAM before block detection is impractical on any workstation. LDxBlocks solves this through the LDxBlocks_backend abstraction: run_Big_LD_all_chr() calls read_chunk(backend, col_idx) to obtain exactly the SNP window it needs, leaving the rest of the genome on disk.

Two file-backed backends handle this streaming access:

Backend Format Package required
"gds" SeqArray GDS BiocManager::install("SeqArray")
"bed" PLINK binary BED install.packages("BEDMatrix")

Both backends are read-only memory-mapped: at any point only the columns requested by the current read_chunk() call are in RAM.

Memory model

Peak RAM with a file-backed backend:

RAMpeak≈n×w×8 bytes\text{RAM}_{\text{peak}} \approx n \times w \times 8 \text{ bytes}

where nn = individuals and ww = subSegmSize. For n=5,000n = 5{,}000 and the default subSegmSize = 1500:

5,000×1,500×8=60 MB per window5{,}000 \times 1{,}500 \times 8 = 60 \text{ MB per window}

Compare to the plain-matrix path: 5,000×10,000,000×8=400 GB5{,}000 \times 10{,}000{,}000 \times 8 = 400 \text{ GB}.

GDS backend (SeqArray)

Installation 

if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
  install.packages("BiocManager")
BiocManager::install("SeqArray")

Converting VCF to GDS

Convert once; use for all subsequent analyses. The GDS format supports random-access compressed storage, making repeated window reads fast:

library(SeqArray)
seqVCF2GDS(
  vcf.fn         = "mydata.vcf.gz",
  out.fn         = "mydata.gds",
  storage.option = "ZIP_RA",   # random-access LZ4 compression
  parallel       = 4L
)

Block detection from GDS 

be_gds <- read_geno("mydata.gds")
be_gds
# LDxBlocks backend
#   Type    : gds
#   Samples : 5000
#   SNPs    : 10241680
#   Chr     : 1, 2, ..., 29

blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(
  be_gds,
  method       = "r2",
  CLQcut       = 0.70,
  leng         = 100L,      # reduce for dense WGS panels
  subSegmSize  = 1500L,     # controls peak RAM
  n_threads    = 16L,
  min_snps_chr = 100L,      # skip scaffold chromosomes
  verbose      = TRUE
)

close_backend(be_gds)

What happens inside read_chunk() for GDS

Each call executes:

SeqArray::seqSetFilter(gds, variant.id = var_ids[col_idx], verbose = FALSE)
dos <- t(SeqArray::seqGetData(gds, "$dosage"))
SeqArray::seqResetFilter(gds, verbose = FALSE)

For subSegmSize = 1500 and a 30-chromosome bovine genome with 10 M markers, this is approximately ⌈10,000,000/1,500⌉≈6,700\lceil 10{,}000{,}000 / 1{,}500 \rceil \approx 6{,}700 GDS reads total.

PLINK BED backend (BEDMatrix)

Installation 

install.packages("BEDMatrix")

Block detection from BED

The .bim and .fam files must exist at the same path stem:

be_bed <- read_geno("mydata.bed")   # reads .bim and .fam automatically
be_bed
# LDxBlocks backend
#   Type    : bed
#   Samples : 2847
#   SNPs    : 658423
#   Chr     : 1, 2, ..., 26

blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(
  be_bed,
  method    = "r2",
  CLQcut    = 0.65,
  n_threads = 8L,
  verbose   = FALSE
)

close_backend(be_bed)

BEDMatrix memory-maps the .bed file at the OS level. Row and column indexing triggers page faults that load only the requested bytes from disk.

Parameter selection for large panels

subSegmSize

The single most important memory parameter. The C++ r² kernel allocates an n×wn \times w genotype window and a w×ww \times w LD matrix:

Individuals (n) subSegmSize (w) Window RAM LD matrix RAM
1,000 1,500 12 MB 18 MB
5,000 1,500 60 MB 18 MB
5,000 5,000 200 MB 200 MB
20,000 1,500 240 MB 18 MB

For very large nn, keep subSegmSize at 1,500 or reduce it. For moderate nn, increasing to 3,000–5,000 reduces GDS reads at the cost of more RAM.

leng

The boundary-scan half-window in SNPs. boundary_scan_cpp() scans 2 * leng columns per candidate cut position, with cost scaling as O(p×leng2)O(p \times \text{leng}^2) per chromosome. For a 500 k-SNP chromosome with leng = 200 this is ~20 billion element comparisons. Reduce leng to 50–100 for very dense panels:

# Dense WGS panel: reduce leng, increase subSegmSize
blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(
  be_gds,
  method      = "r2",
  CLQcut      = 0.70,
  leng        = 50L,      # narrower boundary scan
  subSegmSize = 3000L,    # fewer GDS reads per chromosome
  n_threads   = 16L
)

n_threads

OpenMP thread scaling is efficient up to approximately 8–16 threads for typical window sizes. Benchmark on your hardware:

times <- sapply(c(1L, 2L, 4L, 8L, 16L), function(nt) {
  system.time(
    run_Big_LD_all_chr(be_gds, method = "r2", CLQcut = 0.7,
                        n_threads = nt, verbose = FALSE)
  )["elapsed"]
})
names(times) <- paste0(c(1,2,4,8,16), " threads")
round(times, 1)

Haplotype analysis on large datasets

For large panels, extract haplotypes one chromosome at a time to avoid loading the full genome matrix:

be      <- read_geno("wgs_panel.gds")
blocks  <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(be, method = "r2", CLQcut = 0.70,
                               n_threads = 16L, verbose = FALSE)

# Process chromosome 1 only
chr1_idx    <- which(be$snp_info$CHR == "1")
chr1_geno   <- read_chunk(be, chr1_idx)          # load chr1 only
chr1_blocks <- blocks[blocks$CHR == "1", ]
chr1_info   <- be$snp_info[chr1_idx, ]

haps <- extract_haplotypes(chr1_geno, chr1_info, chr1_blocks, min_snps = 5)
div  <- compute_haplotype_diversity(haps)
feat <- build_haplotype_feature_matrix(haps, top_n = 5, scale_features = TRUE)

close_backend(be)

Troubleshooting

GDS handle errors on Windows. SeqArray GDS file handles are not fork-safe. Avoid FORK-based parallelism (parallel::mclapply()); use n_threads > 1 (OpenMP within a single process) instead.

BEDMatrix and missing data. BEDMatrix codes missing genotypes as NA. These are correctly handled by compute_r2_cpp() via mean imputation per column. No pre-imputation is required.

“fewer than min_snps_chr SNPs” messages. Many reference genomes have hundreds of unplaced scaffolds with a handful of markers. Set min_snps_chr = 100 or higher to skip them automatically.

Out-of-memory on boundary scan. If boundary_scan_cpp() runs out of RAM on very large chromosomes, reduce leng to 30–50. This rarely affects block quality because large-block boundaries are evident even with a narrow scan window.

Very long chromosomes (> 2 M SNPs). For panels with > 2 M SNPs per chromosome, set subSegmSize = 500 and leng = 30. The algorithm will make more GDS reads but use far less RAM per step.

References

  • Lawrence M et al. (2013) PLOS Comput. Biol. 9(8):e1003118. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pcbi.1003118
  • VanRaden PM (2008) J. Dairy Sci. 91(11):4414–4423. https://doi.org/10.3168/jds.2007-0980

On this page
